title: theodorea thomsonii
intro: Theodorea thomsonii, also known as Sandalwood, is a slow-growing, evergreen tree native to the Pacific island region, particularly in Fiji, Samoa, and Vanuatu. The plant is prized for its heartwood, which contains a rich, woody oil with medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Theodorea thomsonii oil include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating skin irritations and wounds. The oil is commonly used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, ointments, and incense sticks, and is also used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
